violations.text: Cover to steam table pan observed on the hand-washing sink at the service line. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Pasta was placed in the walk-in cooler in covered containers greater than four inches deep. Pasta was uncovered and divided into smaller amounts during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

Receiving door is not tight-fitting. Spaces observed in the bottom corner of each side. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/27/2024

Cup, gloves, and visor were observed in the hand-washing sink on the service line. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2025
Spray bottles used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2025

Thermometers are lacking in the refrigeration unit(s). All cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer inside the unit that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2025

The receiving door is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/27/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the commercial mixer and the gaskets of the 6-door reach-in units have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2025

The receiving door is still not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/27/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, commercial mixer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2024

The receiving door is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/27/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of some equipment have an accumulation of food residue and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 01/25/2024

Ambient temperature of walk-in cooler was observed held at 42 degrees Fahrenheit. Cheese in this unit observed at 42 degrees F. Ice observed on the condenser unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Service scheduled for today. Temperature is to be monitored hourly and food products moved if needed. New Violation. Correct By: 09/29/2023

The door to the outside, located in the receiving area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/13/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (deli slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2023

Buffalo chicken patties in hot hold unit #2 were observed held at 119 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Patties were reheated to 165 degrees F during inspection. Hot hold temperatures must be carefully monitored. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2023

The door to the outside, located in the receiving area, and the receiving door itself, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of black/brown residue. Areas are to be properly cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023
